item.title: Black Kite’s Fifth Annual Third-Party Breach Report Highlights Ripple Effects of Cyber-Ecosystem Breaches
item.description: Dive into Black Kite’s 5th annual third-party breach report, outlining ripple effects across cyber ecosystems and strategies to mitigate risk.

item.title: Focus Friday: TPRM Insights on Adobe ColdFusion, Joomla, Apache Superset, and NextCloud Vulnerabilities
item.description: Focus Friday: Learn more about recent cyber breaches and critical vulnerabilities from Black Kite.

item.title: NIST CSF 2.0 IN 10 QUESTIONS
item.description: Set of guidelines designed to help organizations improve cybersecurity practices and manage cybersecurity risks in a consistent and comprehensive manner.

item.title: FOCUS FRIDAY: Enhancing TPRM Vigilance Against Critical Vulnerabilities in FortiOS, Symantec, RoundCube, and QNAP Systems
item.description: Focus Friday: Learn more about recent cyber breaches and critical vulnerabilities from Black Kite.

item.title: Why Your Entire Company Should Help Build Your Third-Party Risk Management Program
item.description: To build a truly effective TPRM program, your entire organization should be involved — from the board of directors to first-week interns.

item.title: Missed Signals: How the Ransomware Attack on Apple’s Supplier Could Have Been Detected Sooner
item.description: Could Apple have seen this ransomware attack coming? Discover the signals that flew under the radar.

item.title: Major Third-Party Data Breaches in March 2021: Airlines, Healthcare & More Take a Hit
item.description: Black Kite counts down the top 7 data breaches caused by third parties in March 2021

item.title: Three Assumptions You Should Never Make About Ransomware
item.description: History repeats itself. Avoid becoming the next ransomware headline by learning from victim's critical mistakes with tips from CSO Bob Maley.

item.title: Do’s & Don’ts for Revamping Your Cybersecurity Playbook
item.description: Do’s & Don’ts for Revamping Your Cybersecurity Playbook

item.title: Why Cybersecurity is a Business Issue (Not Just an IT Issue)
item.description: Building cyber resilience shouldn't just fall on the shoulders of your IT department. Here is why!

item.title: Ransomware Day Strikes Again: What Kaseya Should Teach Us
item.description: Ransomware groups are constantly improving their strategy, but these attacks are not brand new, we must become stronger than the bad actors.

item.title: The “Whac-A-Mole” Game Between Cybercriminals and Security Teams
item.description: By the time we come to understand the full magnitude of one cyberattack, we're already knee deep in another.It's time we act faster.

item.title: Seven Sins in Enterprise Cybersecurity That Will Cost You More in the Long Run
item.description: Chances are, settling for a mediocre cybersecurity strategy today will cost you in the future. Here's what you need to avoid.

item.title: How to Create an Effective Cyber Incident Response Plan
item.description: The 5 steps to an effective incident response plan: Preparation, detection, containment, analysis and recovery.

item.title: The Most Common Cybersecurity Threats Facing Supply Chains in 2021
item.description: As we enter the second half of 2021 and reflect back on the previous six months, it’s clear that all eyes are now on supply chain security.

item.title: Black Kite Added to the Department of Homeland Security’s Continuous Diagnostics and Mitigation Program’s Approved Products List (CDM APL)
item.description: Black Kite is now on the Department of Homeland Security’s CDM Approved Products List, validating its security platform for national risk management efforts.

item.title: Focus Friday: Tackling Emerging Threats – Volt Typhoon, Elementor RCE, and MLflow Vulnerabilities
item.description: Focus Friday: Learn more about recent cyber breaches and critical vulnerabilities from Black Kite.

item.title: Mitigating the Storm: Vendor Risk Management Amidst the Recent Cisco IOS XE Web UI Vulnerability (CVE-2023-20198)
item.description: The critical vulnerability in the Cisco IOS XE Web UI, designated as CVE-2023-20198, has drawn significant attention in the cybersecurity community.

item.title: Due Diligence 5-Step Checklist
item.description: Let’s understand what third-party due diligence is about and walk through the steps for an effective due diligence process.

item.title: Third Party Breach Report Commentary from Jeffrey Wheatman
item.description: What are my thoughts on the insights of last year’s third party attacks? Numbers always tell a story. How will leaders react to that story?

item.title: Don’t Ignore the Smaller Partners in Your Third-Party Risk Ecosystem
item.description: Big vendors you work with aren’t necessarily the riskiest - see why you need to pay attention to your sneaky small third parties (and their vendors).

item.title: No More Best Practice
item.description: ‘We’ve always done it that way’ is no longer justifiable in a world of constant changes and modern cyber risk. Cut best practice, for the better.

item.title: What Is a Brute Force Attack (And How Can You Prevent It)?
item.description: Brute force attacks use speed and smarts to quickly try many passwords - what can you and your team do to prevent this avenue of attack?

item.title: Introducing Black Kite’s New FAIR Model Scenarios – Ransomware and Business Interruption
item.description: In addition to data breaches, learn about the two new FAIR model scenarios Black Kite has added to its CRQ — ransomware and business interruption.

item.title: FOCUS FRIDAY: TPRM INSIGHTS ON IVANTI EPMM, GOANYWHERE, AND VMWARE VULNERABILITIES
item.description: Welcome to Focus Friday: Learn more about recent cyber breaches and critical vulnerabilities from Black Kite.

item.title: FOCUS FRIDAY: A TPRM Deep Dive into Outlook RCE and FortiClient EMS Vulnerabilities
item.description: Spotlight the significant cybersecurity incident at Change Healthcare, a pivotal event that has highlighted the criticality of TPRM in the healthcare.

item.title: Downfall of a Digital Titan: The Global Sting That Toppled LockBit
item.description: LockBit, notorious for its ransomware rampage, has long been the digital boogeyman for businesses worldwide.

item.title: Major Third-Party Data Breaches Revealed in January 2021
item.description: Data breaches in 2020 proved to be just as costly as they were high profile.

item.title: FOCUS FRIDAY: Lockbit Shutdown Insights & ScreenConnect and Exchange Server Vulnerabilities
item.description: Comprehensive look at the cybersecurity landscape, starting with an analysis of the Lockbit ransomware group’s shutdown from a TPRM perspective.

item.title: Is AlphV/BlackCat Staging its Own Death for a Rich Exit?
item.description: AlphV/BlackCat are at it again - Read more about the Change Healthcare breach from Black Kite Research.
